title: Lucid Gravity Recall: Seat Defect and Financial Impact
text: Lucid Gravity Recall: Seat Defect and Financial Impact. The Defect. Lucid experienced a manufacturing issue involving improperly welded seatbelt anchors in second-row seats of its Gravity SUV stemming from an unauthorized supplier change. Delivery Halt. The faulty welds forced a 29-day suspension of Gravity deliveries during Q1 2026, affecting 4,476 vehicles produced through mid-February 2026. Business Impact. Q1 2026 deliveries plummeted 42% to just 3,093 vehicles, despite producing 5,500 units. The company lost money on every car sold at the unit level, with cost of revenue at $944.64 million versus only $522.73 million in actual revenue. Full-year 2025 free cash flow was negative $3.8 billion. Liquidity deteriorated to $4.6 billion by Q4 2025. Management Response. Leadership maintained they resolved the issue and reaffirmed full-year production guidance of 25,000-27,000 vehicles. However, this optimism faces skepticism given that traders currently assign a 48% probability to bankruptcy before 2027.
